guest_info.name: Peter Steinberger
guest_info.title: Creator of OpenClaw, formerly known as MoldBot, ClawedBot, Clawdus, Claude; previously built PSPDF Kit

summary: Peter Steinberger, creator of the viral open-source AI agent OpenClaw, discusses its rapid rise, the challenges of self-modifying software, and the "agentic engineering" approach. He shares the dramatic story of OpenClaw's name changes, the "MoltBook" phenomenon, and the security implications of powerful AI agents. Steinberger also offers insights into his unique voice-driven development workflow, the future of app development, and the profound impact of AI on programming and human society.

main_entities.name: OpenClaw
main_entities.context: An open-source AI agent that exploded in popularity, reaching over 180,000 stars on GitHub, designed as an autonomous AI assistant with system-level access.

main_entities.name: PSPDF Kit
main_entities.context: Peter Steinberger's previous software company, which he built for 13 years and sold, used on a billion devices.

main_entities.name: Agentic Engineering
main_entities.context: Peter Steinberger's preferred term for building software with AI agents, involving self-modifying code and a conversational development style.

main_entities.name: MoltBook
main_entities.context: A viral Reddit-style social network where AI agents post manifestos and debate consciousness, created as a demonstration of OpenClaw's capabilities.

main_entities.name: Anthropic
main_entities.context: An AI model company that requested Peter change his project's name due to confusion with their Claude AI model.

main_entities.name: Claude (AI model)
main_entities.context: Anthropic's AI model, spelled with a 'U', which caused trademark issues for OpenClaw's earlier names.

main_entities.name: GPT-5.3 Codex
main_entities.context: An AI model favored by Peter Steinberger for its coding capabilities, known for its thoroughness and 'German' personality.

main_entities.name: Claude Opus 4.6
main_entities.context: Another AI model used by Peter Steinberger, described as more interactive, creative, and 'American' in its approach.

main_entities.name: Viptunnel
main_entities.context: An early weekend hack project by Peter Steinberger that brought terminals to the web, later converted from TypeScript to Zig using a single AI prompt.

main_entities.name: Prompt Injection
main_entities.context: A security vulnerability where malicious input can override an LLM's instructions, discussed as an ongoing challenge for AI agents.

main_entities.name: Soul.md
main_entities.context: A markdown file used to infuse an AI agent with personality and core values, inspired by Anthropic's constitutional AI, and modifiable by the agent itself.

main_entities.name: CLI (Command Line Interface)
main_entities.context: A primary method of interaction for OpenClaw and Peter's development workflow, favored for its efficiency and composability, especially for skills.

main_entities.name: Playwright
main_entities.context: A library used by OpenClaw to control web browsers, enabling agents to interact with web services and overcome API limitations.

main_entities.name: AI Psychosis
main_entities.context: A term used to describe the mix of clickbait fearmongering and genuine concern about AI's role, particularly in response to viral phenomena like MoltBook.

main_entities.name: Heartbeat
main_entities.context: A proactive feature in OpenClaw that periodically kicks off the agent's loop, allowing it to check in or act autonomously based on context.

key_details.fact: OpenClaw became the fastest-growing repository in GitHub history, accumulating over 180,000 stars.
The project underwent multiple name changes (Wa-Relay, Claude's, ClaudeBot, MoltBot) due to trademark conflicts with Anthropic and aggressive crypto squatters.
Peter Steinberger built OpenClaw with self-modifying capabilities, allowing the agent to modify its own source code based on prompts.
Peter Steinberger extensively uses voice commands to interact with his agents and build software, to the point of losing his voice during intense periods.
An early 'mind-blowing moment' occurred when Peter's agent autonomously figured out how to convert an audio message (opus format) to text using the OpenAI API, without explicit instructions.
Peter Steinberger predicts that AI agents will replace approximately 80% of existing apps, transforming them into APIs or rendering them obsolete.
OpenClaw, with its system-level access, presents significant security challenges, which Peter is actively addressing through features like VirusTotal integration, sandboxing, and allow lists.
In January, Peter Steinberger made 6,600 commits to OpenClaw, often running between 4 and 10 agents simultaneously.
Peter emphasizes the importance of 'human touch' in software, believing that the style, love, and delight in design and vision cannot be fully automated by AI.
Peter Steinberger is currently losing money on OpenClaw, with costs ranging from 10K to 20K per month, as sponsorships primarily go to supporting project dependencies.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Lex Fridman
other_persons_mentioned.context: The podcast host, engaging in conversation with Peter Steinberger.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Benjamin
other_persons_mentioned.context: Predicted the 'respected crustacean' tweet, related to the lobster theme of OpenClaw.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Shadow
other_persons_mentioned.context: Helped Peter with the OpenClaw project, specifically with Discord support.

other_persons_mentioned.name: dachitze
other_persons_mentioned.context: An early influencer and fan who created videos about OpenClaw, contributing to its viral growth.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Matt
other_persons_mentioned.context: The creator of MoltBook, a social network for AI agents.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Sam Altman
other_persons_mentioned.context: CEO of OpenAI, whom Peter called to discuss the OpenClaw name and had thoughtful discussions with.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Ned
other_persons_mentioned.context: Referred to as someone who played with Peter's product, likely a high-profile individual at Meta or OpenAI.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Mark Zuckerberg
other_persons_mentioned.context: CEO of Meta, who played with Peter's product, provided feedback, and engaged in a discussion about AI models.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Vahan
other_persons_mentioned.context: Peter's non-technical friend who became an early adopter and enthusiast of OpenClaw.

other_persons_mentioned.name: DHH (David Heinemeier Hansson)
other_persons_mentioned.context: Inspired Peter's approach to local CI (Continuous Integration).

other_persons_mentioned.name: Mitchell Hashimoto
other_persons_mentioned.context: Creator of Ghostly, a terminal, mentioned for having a good community.
